canada_profile.public_sector_ai_procurement: limited relative to peers — scale and visibility gaps
canada_profile.overall_policy_rank: below USA, UK, Germany, France in overall competitiveness

existing_canadian_frameworks: Pan-Canadian AI Strategy (CIFAR) — talent, research, national institutes
Canadian Sovereign AI Compute Strategy (2024) — compute infrastructure, Canadian data/IP sovereignty
AI Strategy for the Federal Public Service (2025-2027) — federal department AI adoption
AIDA (Artificial Intelligence and Data Act) — proposed, not yet passed
framework_gap: Canada's strategies emphasize capacity building and institutional readiness, but results show these are not yet reflected in measurable outcomes: legislative activity, procurement scale, governance participation, or public literacy.

peer_comparisons.USA: strong implementation through large-scale procurement and deployment
peer_comparisons.UK: most balanced — performs well across governance, research, and implementation
peer_comparisons.Germany: benefits from EU binding frameworks (EU AI Act); higher governance participation
peer_comparisons.France: benefits from EU binding frameworks; regulatory leadership
peer_comparisons.China: large-scale development and state-supported industrial scaling

key_findings: Canada performs strongly in Government Strategy (100/100) and Operating Environment
Canada ranks below USA, UK, Germany, France in overall policy competitiveness
Canada has comparatively low legislative activity and limited public-sector AI procurement
Policy intent has not yet translated into large-scale implementation
Germany and France outperform Canada partly due to EU binding governance frameworks
UK demonstrates balanced execution across governance, research, and implementation
Canada's main gap is not absence of strategy — it is limited conversion of strategy into visible, measurable execution
core_gap: Canada has strong strategy frameworks but lacks visible execution. The challenge is translating policy intent into legislative activity, procurement scale, and governance influence comparable to leading nations.
